slides.label: What we are actually proposing
slides.headline: We'll give 25,000 fans the best experience, and your business the best return.

slides.label: The cost of friction
slides.headline: Right now, friction is singlehandedly responsible for the loss of more than 40% of business.
slides.body: When buying is slow or clunky, fans may not complain about it, they may just abandon the idea of buying. The worst part is, the money does not go elsewhere, it simply remains in their pockets. This is lost revenue the arena will never see.

bars.title: Walk away from the queue
bars.caption: Data from NBA arenas tells us that fans abandon an intent of getting food or merchandising if the line looks too long.

bars.title: Will not leave their seat
bars.caption: From our FC Porto experience, we know that half the fans skip buying because they either refuse to miss the action or fear the size of the line.

slides.label: The cure, and the lever we control
slides.headline: Removing friction smoothens out every business stream.
slides.body: The pattern holds across everything the platform will sell. By improving the buying experience, removing friction, making it faster and easier for the fans, they will buy more. This is the part of the revenue we have in our hands.

items.heading: Food and beverages
items.body: The NBA data shows us there is 42% more F&B sales when the queue is halved. This is the biggest single win, and the one mobile ordering and cashless payments go straight at.

items.heading: Merchandise
items.body: FC Porto data is clear about merchandise: 29% more is spent per order when fans browse and buy from their phone instead of a counter. Even if the counter is queue-less.

items.heading: Click and collect
items.body: Click and collect ties to the two items above: generally, across the board, both F&B and merch sales grow if fans can skip the line and simply pick up their goods.

items.heading: Cashless checkout
items.body: Since introducing cashless payments and reducing friction, the average spent per match increased by over 25%.

slides.label: Why it has to be one system
slides.headline: Those numbers are actually the result of multiple products glued together, carelessly.

slides.label: Why it has to be one system
slides.headline: We have the opportunity to create one system and design the experience end-to-end. We are sure: even better results.

slides.label: The impact of getting it right
slides.headline: Pause. More than one journey, KYC has to be at the core.
slides.body: This one system, one journey approach will naturally impact conversion and spending in itself. However, an intelligent ecosystem takes it one step further (think of Apple): when engineered with a single identity service across all products, we will capture the fan data from the first interaction, keeping them bound all the way through, while targeting information surgically to them. That is the difference between guessing and knowing. This is all about maximising the opportunity. The whole journey, one profile.

bars.title: Service design gains
bars.caption: 29% is the average of the one-system, one-journey gain. But fans stay largely anonymous, and there is no centralised data hub that processes user behavioural data.

bars.title: Expected ecosystem gains
bars.caption: With the one-journey, one-profile approach, we believe we can nearly double any expected gain from disassociated products.

slides.label: Why decide now
slides.headline: Designed and engineered from the beginning.
slides.body: Traditional venue development treats software as a post-construction afterthought, or a puzzle made of pieces from different sources, inheriting severe technical debt or creating unnecessary dependencies with third-party services. Designing an open, unified data framework during the strategy and definition phase avoids the cost penalty of physical retrofitting, refactoring or late-stage software remediation.
slides.valueLabel: The cost of retrofitting data loops, physical sensors, custom integrations, or any other service onto finished products, versus building them as one from day one.

slides.label: LOVE is the answer
slides.headline: How we solve all that? With LOVE.
slides.body: We run everything we build through the same framework: LOVE. We do it multiple times, we do it for each flagship feature, we do it for the product as a whole. The Discovery prototypes allow us to complete our first passes, assessing the product's lovability and overall user experience.

items.heading: Learn
items.body: Can a first-time fan see what is on and what it costs in seconds?

items.heading: Onboard
items.body: Is the first purchase effortless, and does it lead naturally to the next?

items.heading: Value
items.body: Was it a remarkable experience, was it worth their effort and their time?

items.heading: Endorse
items.body: Does it make fans come back, share, and bring others with them?

slides.label: Proof it pays
slides.headline: allO: we built it as one system, and people spent more.
slides.body: We designed allO across every product at once, not screen by screen. The whole customer journey, treated as one thing. When the pieces connect, people move between them and buy more. The cross-sell is not a campaign bolted on afterwards, but rather a strategic move built from day one.

slides.label: Proof it holds up
slides.headline: FC Porto: the same engine, live under real pressure.
slides.body: Some of the features you are scoping today, we have built there. Secondary market, member wallet, buying for family and guests, parking, and one-click checkout with Apple Pay and Google Pay. We built it, and we run it as the club's only ticketing channel. It holds through matchday on-sale, when everyone shows up at once.

metrics.label: Page views in the first season

slides.label: The long game
slides.headline: We are building for 2035.
slides.caption: The arena is built to last decades. The platform should too.
slides.body: More than just solving the 2030 launch, we are building a product that lasts as long as the concrete in the building. A platform you keep growing from, faster and more efficiently.

slides.label: Scope
slides.headline: Both products, built as one.
slides.body: One system and one journey only work if both products are designed together. So we build them side by side, run everything through LOVE, and keep the experience whole.

tabs.label: Discovery
body: This phase starts with an online kick-off and ends with the in-person sessions. Naturally, with the help of AI, we can create near-fully-functional prototypes, in code, just in time to iterate when we meet face-to-face.

During the workshop, with further help of AI, as we discuss and iterate the wireframes live with the clickable prototype, another team member is live-vibe-coding and refining it, allowing us to see the reflection of our discussion in near-real-time.
By the end of the workshop, we have a fully defined and discussed set of wireframes that will serve as the source-of-truth for the scope of work of the project ahead.

deliverables.label: Clickable prototypes of both products
deliverables.description: Built in code. The source of truth that guides design, build and acceptance.

deliverables.label: Validated technical architecture
deliverables.description: Shared identity, integration approach, and the stack, confirmed not assumed.

deliverables.label: Assumptions register
deliverables.description: Every unknown named and carried at a stated risk, not buried in the price.

deliverables.label: Firm scope and estimate
deliverables.description: The build scope and price fixed, within the band in this proposal.

team.description: Map the journey, frame the prototypes.
Technical discovery on the riskiest integrations.

tabs.label: Design
body: The design phase is business as usual. Taking the prototypes from the workshop, we will define the look and feel, create the final design, interactions, micro-animations and design system, wrapping it up with the documentation and handover for engineers.

Now, with AI, through the design phase, as we progress through the pages, we may create prototypes, in code, that fully match the style of the visual design, allowing us to test with real people, using a near real prototype. This AI-generated code (specifically for prototyping) will never be used in production.

deliverables.label: Web and app design systems
deliverables.description: Documented, consistent with each other, configurable for both brands.

deliverables.label: Final clickable prototypes, in code
deliverables.description: Fully designed, with real micro-interactions and animations. What we user-test before build.

deliverables.label: Accessibility built in
deliverables.description: WCAG 2.2 AA designed into every component, not retrofitted.

deliverables.label: LOVE-validated journeys
deliverables.description: Every key path tested against the whole-system standard before build.

team.description: Owns the design systems and visual direction.
Build the per-surface journeys and components.
Keep design build-ready and aligned to the architecture.

tabs.label: Implementation
body: Website and app built in parallel on one foundation, accepted piece by piece. Integration, QA and accessibility run continuously, never left to the end.

deliverables.label: The website and the app, built
deliverables.description: Both products developed in parallel, feature by feature, on a shared foundation.

deliverables.label: All the source code, yours
deliverables.description: Clean and documented, in your repositories from day one. No lock-in.

deliverables.label: Quality assurance at every release
deliverables.description: Dedicated QA against agreed acceptance criteria, plus automated tests on every change.

deliverables.label: Tested under real load
deliverables.description: Payment, ticketing and access flows proven against peak conditions.

team.description: One designer full-time, protected from other studio work.
Parallel web and app tracks on one foundation.

tabs.label: Arena integration
body: The physical arena does not exist until late. So this is where the platform meets the real turnstiles, POS and access hardware, tested and tuned against the working venue.

At this stage, we should be with you, physically in the Arena, very often. This phase is still an active development one and, as you'll see in the next slide, it is not a short phase because, as we test in place, we expect changes to be needed, potentially in both design and code.

deliverables.label: Integration with the physical arena
deliverables.description: Access control, POS, payment gateways, on-site hardware.

deliverables.label: Load and resilience testing
deliverables.description: Against real arena conditions, not synthetic load.

deliverables.label: Stabilisation and operational tuning
deliverables.description: The platform hardened against the working venue.

team.description: Owns the hardware integration and sign-off.
Scaled up around each integration event.

tabs.label: Go-live and hypercare
body: Go-live in December 2030. At this stage, we must be there for the first big events. We need to be present to ensure that if any fixes or changes are needed, we can cater for those in real time.

Then comes Hypercare. We stay close while the platform settles, and we hand it over properly through knowledge-transfer workshops and full documentation. By the time we step back, your team can run and grow it without us.

deliverables.label: Live platform in December 2030
deliverables.description: Production rollout, with final feature acceptance after go-live.

deliverables.label: Independent security audit addressed
deliverables.description: Penetration test findings resolved within scope before launch.

deliverables.label: Three knowledge-transfer workshops
deliverables.description: Content operations, delivery process, and technical handover. Recorded and documented.

team.description: Everyone on deck for rollout and the first events.
Present for go-live and the first high-traffic events.

slides.label: Schedule
slides.headline: Both products, start to finish.
slides.body: As said multiple times before, we will develop both products at the same time, start to finish. This is the only way to ensure cohesion and common knowledge between everyone, having the one system, one journey as our guiding principle.

slides.label: Team
slides.headline: Senior people, start to finish. No juniors.

members.name: Rui Sereno
members.bio: Built the LOVE framework. Keeps the whole thing feeling like one experience, not a pile of separate parts.

members.name: Francisco Marques
members.bio: Owns the shared identity, profile and integration architecture across both surfaces.

members.name: André Resendes
members.bio: Owns the service design and the whole-system approach across web and app.

members.name: Tiago Duarte
members.bio: Keeps the website and app programmes in step. Owns the schedule and release timing.

members.name: Alec Norton
members.bio: Your point of contact. On the hook for keeping you happy across both tenders.

slides.label: Investment
slides.headline: Two tenders, priced on their own.
slides.body: Each product is priced on its own, exactly as the tender asks.

slides.label: Award both
slides.headline: But there's a better way.
slides.body: Built by one team as one system, one Discovery, one foundation, one team. That efficiency is real, so we pass it back: 1,29M€ off.
slides.caption: A real 1,29M€ saving. One Discovery, one foundation, one team.

slides.label: The choice
slides.headline: Designed as one system, the arena and its 25,000 fans both get more.
